stories.title: Eric Swalwell will resign from Congress. And, Trump feuds with Pope Leo over Iran War
stories.description: Eric Swalwell is resigning from Congress after multiple women accused him of sexual assault and misconduct. And, Trump is feuding with Pope Leo, calling him weak on crime for opposing the war.

stories.title: Israel and Lebanon set to meet for first direct talks in more than 30 years
stories.description: An official briefed on Israel's strategy for the talks described Tuesday's meeting as "preparatory" and aimed at laying out a framework for future negotiations.

stories.title: Ukraine hopes for more cooperation after Hungary elects new prime minister
stories.description: The incoming prime minister of Hungary is no fan of Ukraine, but he says he wants to work with the European Union, raising hopes he'll lift a Hungarian veto on a $100 billion EU loan to Kyiv.

stories.title: 'Hanging by a thread': Fertilizer shortage in India deepens due to ongoing Iran war
stories.description: With shipments through the Strait of Hormuz stalled after the Iran war began, fertilizer shortages are deepening across India.

stories.title: A U.S. military blockade of Iran's ports takes effect. What does that mean?
stories.description: A U.S. blockade of Iranian ports has gone into effect Monday, while President Trump claims Iran wants to make a deal.

stories.title: Trump says U.S. will blockade Iranian ports. And, Orbán loses Hungarian election
stories.description: President Trump announced a blockade of Iranian ports after peace talks with Iran collapsed. And, Viktor Orbán concedes defeat after 16 years in power in Hungary.

stories.title: Trump vows to sink Iranian ships approaching a U.S. blockade of Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: President Trump said the U.S. would interdict vessels that had paid what he called an "illegal toll" to Iran to cross the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Lebanon seeks peace, but Hezbollah needs to be convinced first
stories.description: The Lebanese government go into peace talks with limited influence over the group.

stories.title: 'They are striking everywhere' - Paramedics in Lebanon speak to BBC after Israeli strikes
stories.description: BBC Middle East correspondent Hugo Bachega joins paramedics on duty in Nabatieh - a city that was once vibrant, but is now abandoned.

stories.title: Israelis war-weary but most oppose Iran ceasefire, poll suggests
stories.description: The vast majority did not believe Hezbollah or Iran had been severely weakened by weeks of Israeli and US attacks, according to the poll.

stories.title: The US blockade of Iran is a gamble. Will it work?
stories.description: The US military is gambling on the blockade denying Iran vital trade - but questions remain on whether the bet will pay off.

stories.title: Israel steps up strikes on Lebanon and Hezbollah attacks continue as talks loom
stories.description: More than 160 people have been killed since Friday in Lebanon, where Israel is pressing on with its campaign against Hezbollah despite the US-Iran ceasefire.

stories.title: Iran carried out highest number of executions in decades
stories.description: Organisations warn many more people could be executed in the wake of the anti-government protests which swept Iran earlier this year.

stories.title: Starmer says UK will not join Trump's blockade of Iran's ports
stories.description: UK minesweepers and anti-drone capabilities will continue operating in the region, as pressure ramps up to reopen the key shipping route.

stories.title: After Iran talks falter, the big question is what happens next?
stories.description: Twenty-one hours was not enough to end 47 years of hostility between Iran and the US, writes the BBC's Lyse Doucet.

stories.title: We spoke to the man making viral Lego-style AI videos for Iran. Experts say it's powerful propaganda
stories.description: "Slopaganda" is too weak a term to capture how powerful this "highly sophisticated" content is, one expert says.

stories.title: Concerns after satellite provider restricts Iran images following US pressure
stories.description: Planet Labs says it is restricting coverage of Iran and parts of the Middle East indefinitely.

stories.title: Residents of Iraqi Kurdistan in despair: 'We weren't at war with anyone'
stories.description: After a month and a half spent living under the threat of drones, residents of Iraqi Kurdistan are exhausted. Just 24 hours before the ceasefire, a civilian was killed in a drone attack blamed on Iran by Kurdish authorities. In total, around 700 drone and missile attacks have tar...

stories.title: 'Shocking attack': Italian press reacts to Trump's feud with pope
stories.description: US President Donald Trump posted an AI-generated image of himself as a Jesus-like figure on Sunday, drawing widespread criticism even from some religious conservatives who typically support him, before deleting the post on Monday. It came amid his escalating feud with Pope Leo, w...

stories.title: 'Total dehumanisation': French former hostages in Iran give interview
stories.description: French former hostages Cecile Kohler and Jacques Paris have given their first television interview since returning to France from Iran. They had been detained in Iran on espionage charges for almost four years. The pair spoke in detail about their ordeal, and how they managed to ...

stories.title: China's exports slow amid surging costs brought on by Iran war
stories.description: China's exports grew only 2.5% in March from a year earlier, a significantly slowdown caused by the war in Iran. The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has pushed up the cost of materials, and many Chinese factories saw their profits squeezed last month. Also in this edition: hundre...

stories.title: What hopes for Lebanon-Israel talks as Hezbollah urges cancellation?
stories.description: Lebanese and Israeli representatives are set to meet in Washington Tuesday for US-mediated talks on ending the war in Lebanon. However, Lebanese militant group Hezbollah has said will not abide by any agreements that may result from the negotiations and called for them to be canc...

stories.title: US naval blockade begins as Trump vows will destroy Iran boats that approach
stories.description: President Donald Trump warned on Monday that any Iranian "fast-attack" ships that go near a US maritime "blockade" on Iran would be eliminated. The US blockade on vessels entering and departing Iran went into effect at 1400 GMT on Monday.

stories.title: 'No glimmer of hope': Little optimism among Lebanon's displaced as talks with Israel begin
stories.description: Lebanese and Israeli representatives are set to meet in Washington Tuesday for US-mediated talks on ending the war in Lebanon, although Lebanese militant group Hezbollah has said will not abide by any agreements that may result from the negotiations and called for them to be canc...

stories.title: Lebanon and Israel to hold rare direct talks in Washington despite Hezbollah opposition
stories.description: Lebanese and Israeli envoys meet in Washington on Tuesday for the first face-to-face talks in decades between representatives of the two countries, mediated by US Secretary of State Marco Rubio. The Lebanese militant group Hezbollah has warned it will not abide by any agreements ...

stories.title: Global oil demand to plunge amid disruptions caused by war on Iran: IEA
stories.description: The IEA's oil 'demand destruction' report comes after its chief said unnamed countries are hoarding stocks.

stories.title: What we know about US-hosted talks between Israel and Lebanon
stories.description: Officials from Lebanon and Israel are meeting in the US for talks on Tuesday.

stories.title: Israel-Lebanon direct talks in the US: All to know
stories.description: Israel and Lebanon hold first direct talks since 1993, but Hezbollah's rejection threatens the US-brokered effort.

stories.title: Iran slams YouTube ban on pro-Iranian group’s Lego-style AI videos
stories.description: Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesman says the move aims to 'suppress the truth about their illegal war on Iran'.

stories.title: Sanctioned tankers transit Strait of Hormuz amid US blockade
stories.description: Chinese-owned tanker Rich Starry becomes the first sanctioned vessel to exit the Gulf after the US blockade.

stories.title: Russia’s Lavrov visits China as US pressures Iran with Hormuz blockade
stories.description: China and Russia have been vocal critics of the US-Israel war on Iran, an ally of both world powers.

stories.title: World faces food ‘catastrophe’ if Strait of Hormuz disruption persists: FAO
stories.description: Global agriculture is highly exposed to the blockage of waterways, risking higher commodity prices and food inflation.

stories.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 46 of the US-Iran conflict?
stories.description: The US started a blockade on Iranian ports, but Trump said there is still a chance for Tehran to reach a deal.

stories.title: Around 90 detained in New York City protest over US arms sales to Israel
stories.description: Dozens of protesters were detained by police in New York City on Monday during demonstrations calling for the blocking of arms sales to Israel and an end to US military support for its ally. Demonstrators included the anti-war group Jewish Voice for Peace, which said around 90 pe...

stories.title: Israeli troops fire tear gas at schoolchildren in West Bank
stories.description: UMM KHAIR: Israeli forces fired tear gas at Palestinian schoolchildren staging a sit-in on Monday in the occupied West Bank, footage showed, after settlers blocked access to their school. The Israeli military confirmed it had dispersed an “unusual gathering”, but did not specify ...

stories.title: US blockade of Hormuz puts ceasefire to test
stories.description: • Trump says Iranian navy ‘obliterated’, threatens to shoot at ships ‘anywhere close to blockade’; claims Iranian officials called him on Monday; reveals talks failed due to nuclear issue• US president says more countries to participate as Nato allies refuse to join Trump in bloc...

stories.title: Heathrow airport traffic surges as war diverts global flights
stories.description: • London hub sees 10pc jump in rerouted transit passengers; Mideast routes plunge by half as Asia, Africa traffic soars• Spike in jet fuel prices triggers widespread fare, fee increases; airlines ground flights, overhaul schedules LONDON: Heathrow Airport in London saw a 10 per c...

stories.title: ‘I thought it was me as a doctor’: Trump on AI image of himself as Jesus-like figure
stories.description: US President Donald Trump said on Monday that a picture that he posted on Truth Social, which depicted him as a Jesus-like figure and has now been deleted, was actually supposed to show him as a doctor. “I did post it, and I thought it was me as a doctor, and had to do with the R...

stories.title: Luxury brands face profit squeeze as Iran conflict shrinks UAE mall sales, sources say
stories.description: Sales at Europe’s biggest luxury brands have shrunk in Dubai and Abu Dhabi as the Iran conflict hit the sector’s fastest-growing market in the latest setback for the $400 billion industry whose value has contracted over the last three years. Luxury brands in March reported sales ...

stories.title: U.S., Iran may meet again in Islamabad for second round of talks by next week: Report
stories.description: The contacts are ongoing for the revival of stalled negotiations between the U.S. and Iran, and talks may be held again before the end of the two-week ceasefire on April 21, says reports citing highly placed sources

stories.title: Israel-Iran war LIVE: France, U.K. to co-host Hormuz shipping security talks
stories.description: Pakistan proposes new U.S.-Iran talks as Vance and Trump hint at progress

stories.title: Trump’s genocidal threat to Iran and an elusive deal
Made lot of progress, ball in Iran’s court: Vance
stories.description: The U.S. and Iran failed to reach an agreement in the 21-hour marathon peace talks in Islamabad over the weekend, with Washington insisting that Tehran refused to give up its right over enrichment of nuclear fuel

stories.title: U.S., Iran leave door open to dialogue after tense Islamabad talks
stories.description: Despite numerous obstacles to ​peace, both sides appear to have strong reasons to consider de-escalation

stories.title: Donald Trump vows U.S. will sink any Iran boats that challenge blockade
stories.description: The U.S. military said the blockade would begin at 1930 hours IST and apply to all ships leaving or seeking to dock at Iranian harbours, but as the deadline passed there was no announcement that the measure had come into effect, nor sign of any interceptions

stories.title: Internet blackout in Iran lasts over 45 days — NetBlocks
stories.description: Internet connectivity in Iran has been almost completely disrupted for more than 1,080 hours

stories.title: IN BRIEF: What we know about situation around US blockade of Hormuz Strait
stories.description: Baghdad Today reported that Iran had anticipated a US blockade of its ports and therefore moved dozens of oil-laden tankers out to sea in advance

stories.title: Chinese MFA suggests that US Vice President make efforts to reconcile with Iran
stories.description: According to Chinese Foreign Ministry Spokesman Guo Jiakun, all sides engaged in hostilities should fully comply with the ceasefire conditions, "continue to resolve the conflict through political and diplomatic measures"

stories.title: Russian ambassador to Iran offers condolences over Iranian deaths in US, Israeli strikes
stories.description: According to Abbas Masjadi, head of Iran's Forensic Medicine Organization, more than 3,000 Iranian citizens have fallen victim to the strikes carried out by the United States and Israel
